notes: Spec asserts the user-observable contract for an ESC-cancelled turn: ready→working→ready (the cancelled turn settles back to ready, NOT stuck in working), then a recovery prompt drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on the SAME session, never entering waiting. WHAT GEMINI WRITES ON ESC (v0.46.0): handleUserCancelledEvent flushes any pending item then appends addItem({type:"info",text:"Request cancelled."}); the useHistoryManager chat-recording switch records info/error/warning notices via chatRecordingService.recordMessage but BREAKS for user/gemini, and processGeminiStreamEvents returns UserCancelled→the caller early-returns before the post-stream gemini recordMessage. So exactly one type:"info" 'Request cancelled.' MessageRecord lands on disk for the cancelled turn and NO terminal gemini message does. FIX (#659): the gemini-cli parser's parseInfo now maps the type:"info" cancel notice ('Request cancelled.') to turn_done, so an ESC-cancelled turn settles working→ready instead of sticking in working until the next turn. Before #659 the parser dispatched strictly on type (user/gemini handled, default incl. info → Skip) and inferred turn_done only from a final text-only gemini message, so the cancel notice was dropped and the session stuck in working until the recovery turn's gemini message landed — the known_failing case this cell formerly demonstrated. The 2026-06-13 recording (irrlichd 0.5.1+68aba13, this branch) confirms the fix: the SAME session went ready→working→ready at the cancel (settling ~3s after the 'Request cancelled.' notice was flushed to the chat file), then ready→working→ready for the recovery turn, never entering waiting. The interrupt settle is anchored to turn_start with no wall-clock bound because the cancel-notice flush latency is a property of what the agent writes (the recipe sleeps 8s before the ESC and gemini took ~8s to flush the notice); the meaningful assertion is that the cancelled turn settles to ready on the SAME session BEFORE the recovery turn — matching the sibling turn-aborted-by-error spec, which likewise carries no max_delay_ms on its settle phase. Recorded via the INTERACTIVE driver (gemini -y under tmux: send, sleep, keys:Escape, sleep, recovery send, wait_turn, trailing sleep) so the process outlives the observation window; the ESC is delivered via the keys step (tmux send-keys Escape), which the gemini-cli driver implements.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch
Launcher PID bound by cwd shortly after launch
Long-essay prompt drives ready → working before the ESC interrupt

ESC cancels the in-flight turn; the cancel notice (type:"info" 'Request cancelled.') drives the SAME session working → ready — state does NOT stick in working post-interrupt. Since #659 the parser maps the cancel notice to turn_done, so the cancelled turn settles to ready (in the 2026-06-13 recording ~3s after the notice was flushed to the chat file) BEFORE the recovery turn. No wall-clock bound: the cancel-notice flush latency is a property of what gemini writes (the recipe sleeps 8s before the ESC), so the meaningful assertion is the same-session settle ordering, matching the sibling turn-aborted-by-error settle phase.
Recovery prompt drives ready → working, proving the parser reset after the interrupt
Recovery turn completes back to ready; clean ready → working → ready arc, never entered waiting
